description: A pole with a flat blade for rowing a boat; the tool that moves water to move vessel. 'Rewa' names what reaches through water—effort becoming motion.
sound_symbolism: The word reaches through. 'Re' extends outward, oar entering water. Then 'wa' moves water, vessel gliding forward.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'rewa' (the oar). Human-powered water travel. Works with effort unlike 'phora' (sail) which works with wind.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Effort and patience—each stroke moves forward, journey accomplished through persistent action.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Oars powered journeys—the patient stroke that moves through water, human effort becoming travel.
